overview.subtitle: What is the BBA+LLB Programme?
paragraphs: The BBA+LLB is a dual-degree integrated program that saves students one academic year. It is explicitly designed for students who want to dominate the corporate legal sector. The program merges the core principles of Business Administration (HR, Finance, Marketing) with the strict, analytical frameworks of Indian and International Law.

Approved by the Bar Council of India (BCI), the pedagogy shifts away from pure textbook learning. Students are trained through highly competitive Moot Courts (simulated trials), client counseling sessions, and mandatory 20-week internships spanning trial courts, appellate courts, and corporate law firms.

scope.body: A BBA+LLB graduate is uniquely positioned to understand both the business objectives of a company and the legal boundaries it must operate within. They are aggressively recruited by Top Tier Law Firms (Khaitan & Co, Amarchand Mangaldas), Corporate Legal Departments (as In-House Counsel), Legal Process Outsourcing (LPO) companies, and Investment Banks. Alternatively, graduates can directly enroll with the Bar Council and practice as independent litigation advocates in High Courts and the Supreme Court.
scope.subtitle: Expanding opportunities in corporate law and litigation

curriculum.subtitle: A BCI-mandated curriculum blending business with justice
curriculum.introNote: The first two years heavily integrate business management subjects with foundational law, transitioning into pure, advanced legal frameworks in the final three years.
curriculum.outroNote: Students must complete 20 weeks of mandatory internships across NGOs, Trial Courts, High Courts, and Law Firms.

accordionSections.title: Course Structure Overview
accordionSections.content: The 5-year BBA+LLB program spans 10 semesters. Years 1 and 2 introduce business concepts (Accounting, Marketing) alongside basic law (Torts, Contracts). Years 3, 4, and 5 dive deeply into substantive and procedural laws (IPC, CrPC, Evidence Act), culminating in intensive Clinical Legal Education (Drafting, Moot Courts) and final internships.

accordionSections.title: Year-1: Foundation of Business & Law
accordionSections.content: Principles of Management, Managerial Economics, Legal Methods, Law of Torts, Law of Contracts I, English & Legal Language, Financial Accounting.

accordionSections.title: Year-2: Connecting Management to Justice
accordionSections.content: Human Resource Management, Law of Contracts II, Constitutional Law I & II, Family Law I (Hindu Law), Family Law II (Muslim Law), Jurisprudence.

accordionSections.title: Year-3: Substantive Legal Frameworks
accordionSections.content: Law of Crimes I (IPC), Corporate Law / Company Law, Property Law, Administrative Law, Labour & Industrial Law I, Public International Law.

accordionSections.title: Year-4: Procedural Laws & Evidence
accordionSections.content: Law of Crimes II (CrPC), Civil Procedure Code (CPC) & Limitation Act, Law of Evidence, Taxation Law, Environmental Law, Labour Law II.

accordionSections.title: Year-5: Clinical Papers & Specialization
accordionSections.content: Drafting, Pleading and Conveyancing (Clinical), Moot Court Exercise and Internship (Clinical), Professional Ethics (Clinical), Alternative Dispute Resolution (Clinical), Intellectual Property Rights (IPR), Cyber Law.

Merit scholarship is also offered based on 12th Board marks (Any stream).
Tuition and fees may be net once your scholarship criteria is evaluated.
If a student fails to maintain the required academic standards, they may lose scholarship eligibility.

applySteps.subtitle: Simple 5-step process to join the BBA-LLB programme

items.a: Candidates must have passed 10+2 from a recognized board with a minimum of 50% marks. Students from ANY stream (Arts, Commerce, or Science) are eligible to apply for this course.
items.q: What is the eligibility criteria? Can Arts/Science students apply?

items.a: It is a 5-year (10 semester) integrated degree program. The tuition fee is ₹45,000 per semester.
items.q: What is the duration and fee structure?

items.a: A traditional LLB is a 3-year course done after graduation (total 6 years). BBA+LLB is a 5-year integrated course done directly after 12th, saving you one full academic year.
items.q: Why should I choose 5-Year Integrated LLB over a 3-Year LLB?

items.a: BA+LLB integrates Arts subjects (History, Political Science) with law, making it great for UPSC/Litigation. BBA+LLB integrates Business subjects (Management, Accounts) making it the absolute best choice for Corporate Law.
items.q: What is the difference between BBA-LLB and BA-LLB?

items.a: Yes! The program is fully approved by the Bar Council of India (BCI). Upon graduation, you are eligible to enroll with your State Bar Council and practice in any court in India.
items.q: Is this degree approved by the Bar Council of India (BCI)?

items.a: Graduates are hired by top Law Firms, Corporate Legal Departments, Banks, and LPO companies. You can also practice independently or attempt the Judicial Services Examination to become a judge.
items.q: What kind of jobs will I get after BBA-LLB?

items.a: Yes, we strongly encourage applicants with CLAT or LSAT scores, and they are eligible for heavy scholarships. However, non-CLAT students can also apply via the CPU entrance test.
items.q: Do I need a CLAT score to get admission?

faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about the BBA-LLB programme

roadmap.subtitle: Your journey from high school to a corporate legal expert.

years.contentDesc: The first year introduces you to the corporate world through management principles, alongside the foundational pillars of law like Contracts and Torts.
The second year delves deeply into the Constitution of India, understanding human rights, and the intricacies of Hindu and Muslim Family Law.
The third year focuses heavily on Substantive Laws—learning the Indian Penal Code (IPC), Corporate/Company Law, and Property transfer laws.
Year four moves from 'what is the law' to 'how to apply it in court'. Master the Criminal Procedure Code (CrPC), Civil Procedure (CPC), and Evidence Act.
The final year is entirely clinical. You will draft real legal documents, participate in national Moot Courts, and complete extensive mandatory internships.

list.name: Neha Agarwal
list.text: Combining Business Administration with Law was the best decision. I now handle corporate compliance and mergers for a top firm. CPU's corporate law faculty is excellent.

list.name: Aman Gupta
list.text: The mandatory court internships and rigorous clinical drafting classes gave me the exact practical exposure I needed to start my independent practice confidently.

list.name: Priya Sharma
list.text: The Moot Court competitions completely eradicated my stage fear. Learning to research case laws using SCC Online prepared me for the high-pressure LPO sector.

overview.subtitle: What is the BA+LLB Programme?
paragraphs: The BA+LLB is an integrated dual-degree program that combines a Bachelor of Arts (BA) with a Bachelor of Laws (LLB). This course is specifically tailored for students who have a deep interest in social sciences and public administration, making it the absolute best choice for those preparing for the UPSC (Civil Services) or State Judiciary Examinations.

By studying subjects like Sociology, Political Science, and History alongside Constitutional Law, IPC, and CrPC, students develop a holistic understanding of how laws are formed and how they impact society. The program is heavily focused on public speaking, debate, policy-making, and courtroom advocacy.

scope.body: A BA+LLB degree offers unparalleled opportunities in public service and litigation. Graduates are thoroughly prepared to crack the Judicial Services Exam to become Civil Judges or Magistrates. The strong humanities base makes it an ideal pre-requisite for cracking the UPSC exams (IAS/IPS). Furthermore, graduates establish successful independent practices in High Courts and the Supreme Court as Criminal or Civil Litigation Lawyers, or join top NGOs and Think Tanks as Public Policy Advisors.
scope.subtitle: Expanding opportunities in judiciary, civil services, and litigation

curriculum.subtitle: A BCI-mandated curriculum blending humanities with justice
curriculum.introNote: The first two years heavily integrate Arts subjects (History, Pol Science) with foundational law, transitioning into pure procedural laws in the final three years.
curriculum.outroNote: Students must complete 20 weeks of mandatory internships across NGOs, Trial Courts, Appellate Courts, and Law Firms.

accordionSections.title: Course Structure Overview
accordionSections.content: The 5-year BA+LLB program spans 10 semesters. Years 1 and 2 introduce Arts concepts (Sociology, Political Science) alongside basic law (Torts, Contracts). Years 3, 4, and 5 dive deeply into substantive and procedural laws (IPC, CrPC, Evidence Act), culminating in intensive Clinical Legal Education (Drafting, Moot Courts) and final internships.

accordionSections.title: Year-1: Foundation of Humanities & Law
accordionSections.content: Political Science I, Sociology I, Legal Methods, Law of Torts, Law of Contracts I, English & Legal Language, Legal History.

accordionSections.title: Year-2: Connecting Society to Justice
accordionSections.content: Political Science II, Sociology II, Law of Contracts II, Constitutional Law I & II, Family Law I (Hindu Law), Family Law II (Muslim Law), Jurisprudence.

accordionSections.title: Year-3: Substantive Legal Frameworks
accordionSections.content: Law of Crimes I (IPC), Corporate Law / Company Law, Property Law, Administrative Law, Labour & Industrial Law I, Public International Law.

accordionSections.title: Year-4: Procedural Laws & Evidence
accordionSections.content: Law of Crimes II (CrPC), Civil Procedure Code (CPC) & Limitation Act, Law of Evidence, Taxation Law, Environmental Law, Human Rights Law.

accordionSections.title: Year-5: Clinical Papers & Court Practice
accordionSections.content: Drafting, Pleading and Conveyancing (Clinical), Moot Court Exercise and Internship (Clinical), Professional Ethics (Clinical), Alternative Dispute Resolution (Clinical), Intellectual Property Rights (IPR), Criminology & Penology.

llb.title: LLB (Bachelor of Laws)
llb.eligibility: Graduation in any discipline (B.A./B.Sc./B.Com/B.Tech etc.) with minimum 50% marks from a recognized university
llb.description: An intensive, 3-year BCI-approved professional law degree designed for graduates aiming to become elite litigators, corporate advocates, or judicial officers.
llb.metaTitle: LLB (Bachelor of Laws) Course | Legal Studies | CPU
llb.metaDescription: Pursue a 3-year LLB degree at Career Point University. BCI approved core legal curriculum tailored for graduates. Focus on litigation, drafting, and Judiciary exams.

hero.description: Transform your graduation into a powerful legal career. Master the Indian Penal Code, civil procedures, and constitutional law through pure, unadulterated legal training.

accomplishments.heading: Department's Legal Accomplishments & Impact
accomplishments.trustBadge: ✓ Approved by the Bar Council of India (BCI)

overview.subtitle: What is the 3-Year LLB Programme?
paragraphs: The 3-Year LLB is a pure, unadulterated law program meant for students who have already completed their graduation in another discipline. Unlike the 5-year integrated programs, this course strips away foundational arts or business subjects and plunges students directly into intense legal study from day one.

The curriculum is extremely rigorous, focusing on the deep mechanics of Indian and International law. Students spend their time mastering the Indian Penal Code (IPC), Civil Procedure Code (CPC), and Evidence Act. The program mandates heavy clinical training, meaning students will draft live court petitions, conduct mock cross-examinations in Moot Courts, and intern under senior practicing advocates.

scope.body: An LLB degree holds immense power and respect. Upon graduation, students are eligible to enroll with the State Bar Council and immediately begin independent practice as Litigating Advocates in District Courts, High Courts, and the Supreme Court. It is the primary qualification required to appear for the Judicial Services Examination (to become a Civil Judge) and the Judge Advocate General (JAG) branch in the Indian Army. Graduates are also hired as Legal Advisors in banks, real estate firms, and media houses.
scope.subtitle: Expanding opportunities in litigation, judiciary, and corporate advisory

curriculum.subtitle: A hardcore legal curriculum designed for courtroom dominance
curriculum.introNote: The 3-year LLB focuses entirely on substantive and procedural laws, with no basic graduation subjects mixed in.
curriculum.outroNote: A mandatory 12-week intensive internship in trial courts, appellate courts, or with senior advocates is required to graduate.

accordionSections.title: Course Structure Overview
accordionSections.content: The 3-year LLB spans 6 semesters. Year 1 sets the heavy foundation of Constitutional Law, Contracts, and Crimes. Year 2 shifts focus to how courts operate with procedural laws like CrPC and Evidence. Year 3 is highly clinical, focusing on drafting actual court documents, professional ethics, and mandatory court internships.

accordionSections.title: Semester-1
accordionSections.content: Constitutional Law I, Law of Contracts I, Law of Torts & Consumer Protection, Family Law I (Hindu Law), Law of Crimes I (Indian Penal Code). Total Credits: 24

accordionSections.title: Semester-2
accordionSections.content: Constitutional Law II, Law of Contracts II (Special Contracts), Family Law II (Muslim Law), Property Law & Transfer of Property Act, Public International Law. Total Credits: 24

accordionSections.title: Semester-3
accordionSections.content: Jurisprudence, Administrative Law, Labour & Industrial Law I, Company Law, Law of Crimes II (Criminal Procedure Code - CrPC). Total Credits: 24

accordionSections.title: Semester-4
accordionSections.content: Law of Evidence, Civil Procedure Code (CPC) & Limitation Act, Labour & Industrial Law II, Taxation Law, Environmental Law. Total Credits: 24

accordionSections.title: Semester-5
accordionSections.content: Drafting, Pleading and Conveyancing (Clinical), Professional Ethics & Bar Bench Relations (Clinical), Alternative Dispute Resolution (ADR), Intellectual Property Rights (IPR). Total Credits: 24

accordionSections.title: Semester-6
accordionSections.content: Moot Court Exercise and Internship (Clinical), Cyber Law, Interpretation of Statutes, Human Rights Law & Practice, Penology & Victimology. Total Credits: 24

admissionCriteria.text: Admission based on Graduation marks, followed by Personal Interview / CPU Entrance Test.

notes: Merit scholarship is offered based on aggregate marks obtained in the qualifying Graduation degree.
Tuition and fees may be net once your scholarship criteria is evaluated.
Special scholarships exist for defense personnel and wards of martyrs.
If a student fails to maintain the required academic standards, they may lose scholarship eligibility.

overview.subtitle: What is the LLM Programme?
paragraphs: The Master of Laws (LLM) is an advanced postgraduate degree designed for law graduates who wish to achieve subject-matter mastery in a specific area of law. While an LLB teaches you what the law is and how to apply it, an LLM teaches you the profound philosophy, history, and the future trajectory of the law.

This program shifts away from basic trial advocacy and moves heavily into appellate-level arguments, policy drafting, and intensive legal research. It is the gold standard for advocates who want to join elite corporate law firms as specialized counsels, clear the UGC-NET for a career in academics, or write the higher judicial services exams.

scope.body: An LLM transforms a general lawyer into a specialized legal expert. Graduates are highly sought after by top-tier law firms for niche roles like IPR Consultants, M&A Strategists, and Cyber Law Advisors. Furthermore, an LLM is the mandatory prerequisite for appearing in the UGC-NET to become an Assistant Professor of Law. It also provides a massive analytical advantage for those appearing for the Civil Judge and Higher Judicial Services (HJS) examinations, or those seeking clerkships at the Supreme Court of India.
scope.subtitle: Expanding opportunities in judiciary, academics, and specialized corporate advisory

curriculum.subtitle: An advanced BCI-mandated curriculum for legal mastery
curriculum.introNote: The LLM curriculum is divided into foundational papers on advanced jurisprudence, followed by deep dives into your chosen legal specialization.
curriculum.outroNote: A mandatory legal research dissertation (thesis) must be submitted and defended to complete the degree.

accordionSections.title: Course Structure Overview
accordionSections.content: The LLM program focuses heavily on seminar-style learning and independent research. The initial phase covers mandatory papers for all students regarding advanced legal theories and how to conduct valid legal research. The latter half is entirely dedicated to the student's chosen specialization and the drafting of a comprehensive legal thesis.

accordionSections.title: Semester-1: Core Legal Foundations
accordionSections.content: Law and Social Transformation in India, Indian Constitutional Law: The New Challenges, Judicial Process, Legal Education and Research Methodology.

accordionSections.title: Semester-2: Specialization Dive
accordionSections.content: Specialization Paper I, Specialization Paper II, Specialization Paper III, Practical (Law Teaching & Clinical Work).

accordionSections.title: Semester-3: Advanced Specialization
accordionSections.content: Specialization Paper IV, Specialization Paper V, Specialization Paper VI, Doctrinal Research (Synopsis Submission).

accordionSections.title: Semester-4: Research & Culmination
accordionSections.content: Master's Dissertation (Research, Writing, and Final Defense), Viva-Voce.

accordionSections.title: Available Specializations
accordionSections.content: 1. Corporate and Commercial Law, 2. Criminal Law and Criminal Justice Administration, 3. Constitutional and Administrative Law, 4. Intellectual Property Rights (IPR).

admissionCriteria.text: Admission through CLAT PG scores, LLB Graduation marks, followed by Personal Interview / CPU Entrance Test.

notes: Heavy scholarships are awarded to students with high CLAT PG scores.
Merit scholarship is also offered based on aggregate marks obtained in the qualifying LLB degree.
Tuition and fees may be net once your scholarship criteria is evaluated.
If a student fails to maintain the required academic/research standards, they may lose scholarship eligibility.
scholarships.subtitle: Scholarship for Session 2026-27

applySteps.subtitle: Simple 5-step process to join the LLM programme

items.a: Candidates MUST hold an LLB degree (either the 3-year or the 5-year integrated program) with a minimum of 50% marks from a university recognized by the Bar Council of India. Graduates of other disciplines cannot apply.
items.q: What is the eligibility criteria? Can a non-law graduate apply?

items.a: Depending on the specific UGC and BCI guidelines adopted, the LLM is typically offered as a 1-Year or 2-Year program. The tuition fee is ₹40,000 per semester.
items.q: What is the duration and fee structure?

items.a: An LLB teaches you the basics of all laws and qualifies you to practice in court. An LLM is an advanced master's degree that makes you a specialist in one specific area (like Corporate Law) and qualifies you for academic teaching.
items.q: What is the difference between LLB and LLM?

items.a: We offer highly sought-after specializations including Corporate and Commercial Law, Criminal Law, Constitutional Law, and Intellectual Property Rights (IPR).
items.q: What specializations are available in this LLM?

items.a: Yes, submitting a deeply researched Master's Dissertation (Thesis) is a mandatory academic requirement. You must defend your thesis before a panel to obtain your degree.
items.q: Is a research thesis mandatory?

items.a: LLM graduates are hired as Assistant Professors in law universities, specialized Legal Counsels in top corporate firms (like Amarchand Mangaldas), and policy advisors. Many also use the degree's rigorous training to successfully crack the Judicial Services exams.
items.q: What kind of jobs will I get after LLM?

items.a: Yes, completing an LLM with at least 55% marks makes you fully eligible to appear for the UGC-NET exam, which is required to become an Assistant Professor.
items.q: Can I appear for UGC-NET Law after this?

faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about the LLM programme

roadmap.subtitle: Your journey from a general advocate to a specialized legal expert.

years.contentDesc: The first phase elevates your analytical thinking, focusing on advanced constitutional law, the philosophy of law (jurisprudence), and how to conduct appellate-level research.
The final phase is intensely focused on your chosen specialty (Corporate, Criminal, Const.), preparing for UGC-NET, and defending your Master's thesis.

list.name: Prof. Aman Gupta

community.label: Community Action
description: The School of Legal Studies is deeply committed to societal welfare. Our Legal Aid Clinic provides free legal counseling to underprivileged sections of society, giving students real-world exposure to civil and criminal grievances.
Through Mock Parliaments, Debate Societies, and Legal Literacy Camps in rural areas, our students learn that law is not just a profession, but a tool for social engineering and justice.

gallery.caption: Students at a Rural Legal Aid Camp

list.title: Advanced Moot Court Hall
list.desc: An exact replica of a High Court room designed for simulating trials, cross-examinations, and legal arguments.

list.title: Digital Law Library
list.desc: Access to premium legal databases like SCC Online, Manupatra, and LexisNexis for comprehensive legal research.

list.title: Legal Aid Clinic
list.desc: A dedicated campus facility where students provide free legal advice to the local community under expert supervision.

list.title: Seminar & Debate Halls
list.desc: Equipped with advanced AV systems to host Mock Parliaments, client counseling sessions, and guest lectures by judges.

list.name: Priya Sharma
list.text: The Moot Court competitions completely eradicated my stage fear. It taught me how to research case laws and argue effectively in front of real judges.

list.name: Aman Gupta
list.text: The mandatory court internships arranged by the university gave me the exact practical exposure I needed to start my independent practice.

list.name: Neha Agarwal
list.text: Combining Business Administration with Law was the best decision. I now handle corporate compliance, mergers, and legal drafting for a top tech firm.
